steps.title: Clear the denominator
steps.content: Start with the given equation: $$36=\frac{2K}{v^2}$$ Multiply both sides by $v^2$ to move the variable out of the denominator: $$36v^2=2K$$

steps.title: Isolate the speed term
steps.content: Divide both sides by $36$ to isolate $v^2$: $$v^2=\frac{2K}{36}$$ This gives the square of the speed in terms of the kinetic energy $K$.

steps.title: Take the positive root
steps.content: The problem states that $v$ is positive, so use the positive square root: $$v=\sqrt{\frac{2K}{36}}$$ This matches choice A. The answer is A.
